commit.message:
fix: only emit components that are needed for css modules at the time of emission (#214)

* fix: restore inline CSS module emission and align inventory with rendered components

The previous PR (#212) moved <style type="module"> emission from inline
(inside each component's light DOM) to a blanket emission in <head> for
all inventoried components, and used document.querySelectorAll + manual
CSSStyleSheet construction for SPA adoption. This had two problems:

1. Bloated SSR HTML with style definitions for every route-reachable
   component, including those not rendered on the current page.
2. document.querySelectorAll cannot pierce shadow DOM boundaries, so
   inline <style type="module"> tags were invisible to the framework.

## What this changes

### SSR: restore inline CSS module emission (handler)

emit_css_module() places the <style type="module" specifier="X"> tag
inside the component's light DOM on first render:

  <my-comp><style type="module" specifier="my-comp">CSS</style><template ...>

The browser registers the CSS module globally from this tag and
automatically adopts it via shadowrootadoptedstylesheets on the
declarative shadow root. No JS needed during SSR hydration.

### SSR: emit templates and inventory for rendered components only

Previously, body_end expanded rendered_components into a "reachable"
set that included components inside unrendered <if>/<for> blocks. This
caused inventory to cover components whose <style type="module">
definitions were never emitted (because emit_css_module only fires for
actually-rendered components), creating a mismatch.

Now body_end emits template IIFEs and builds the inventory from
rendered_components only. Components inside unrendered conditional
blocks are excluded — they arrive via templateStyles[] + templates[]
during SPA partial navigation. This ensures: if a component is in
the inventory, both its template IIFE and its CSS module definition
are in the document.

### SPA: use import() with { type: "css" } for style adoption (framework)

For SPA-created components (no declarative shadow root), the framework
uses the browser's CSS module registry directly:

  import(specifier, { with: { type: "css" } })

This returns the browser-registered CSSStyleSheet — no DOM queries,
no manual CSSStyleSheet construction, no application-level cache.
The browser's module registry is the cache (O(1) hash map lookup).

SSR-hydrated components skip this entirely — the browser already
adopted the sheet from shadowrootadoptedstylesheets.

## Files changed

- **crates/webui-handler/src/lib.rs:** Restore emit_css_module() inline
  emission. Emit templates + inventory from rendered_components only
  (not expanded reachable set). Move inventory meta tag to body_end.
  Remove collect_reachable_components (dead code).
- **packages/webui-framework/src/element/styles.ts:** Replace
  querySelectorAll + new CSSStyleSheet() + sheetCache with
  import(specifier, { with: { type: "css" } }) for SPA path.
  SSR path is a no-op (browser handles adoption).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Feedback

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix CLI receive loop exception handling and server CliService race condition

- ConsoleOutputRunner: catch IOException, DeserializationException, and RuntimeException
  separately in the receive loop so any error is shown in the terminal instead
  of silently killing the thread; outer catch also prints to terminal
- CliAppender: synchronize logEvents access in append() and addSink() to prevent
  ConcurrentModificationException when append() modifies the deque while addSink()
  is iterating it during a new CLI connection
- CliService: catch Exception (not just IOException) so an unexpected runtime
  error during connection setup does not kill the accept loop permanently
- Bump mechrain-server 1.0.5 -> 1.0.6
- Bump mechrain-cli 1.0.4 -> 1.0.5

as a direct dependency lets ncc bundle it into dist/index.js.

binaries via __complete, so we only need completion for the standalone

* feat: skip pod CIDR assignment when manageCniPlugin is false

The controller now skips pod CIDR allocation for nodes in sites with
manageCniPlugin set to false. The podCidrAssignments entries are still
required to define the CIDR ranges for inter-site routing, but the
controller will not assign per-node pod CIDRs or modify node specs.

This ensures that sites using an external CNI plugin (e.g. Cilium,
Calico) do not have their pod CIDRs overwritten by the controller.

[windowsCI] Remove hardcoded MKL pin from CI requirements to avoid conflicts with Torch XPU wheel (#3241)

Remove the hardcoded MKL installation line from the Windows CI workflow
so that the CI does not force-install MKL versions that can conflict
with the locally-built Torch XPU wheel.

The change updates the Windows workflow file to remove lines mentioning
mkl from the requirements before installing them.

Changed file:
`torch-xpu-ops/.github/workflows/_windows_ut.yml`

- Skip wb.Close for the host workbook so the macro keeps running

fix: ramp arrow icon not matching instruction modifier (#406)

* fix: ramp arrow icon not matching instruction modifier (#255)

The leaflet-routing-machine helper _leftOrRight, used when converting
on/off ramp steps, was purely binary: anything that didn't contain
'left' was returned as 'Right'. This meant an 'on ramp straight'
maneuver ended up with modifier 'Right', rendering a right-turn arrow
even though the instruction text said 'straight'.

Monkey-patch _leftOrRight on the router instance so that modifiers
containing neither 'left' nor 'right' (e.g. 'straight') are passed
through unchanged. 'straight' then becomes the 'Straight' modifier and
getIconName correctly maps it to the continue (straight) arrow icon.

Also add a dedicated test suite covering all modifier cases including
the straight regression.

gps: 14 Hz output rate, 1 Hz PPS timepulse

GPS output rate 16->14 Hz (GPS_NOMINAL_MS 63->71 ms): at 16 Hz the
chip was missing ~20-24 samples per 256-sample buffer (~14.6 Hz
actual with multi-constellation). 14 Hz sits safely below the
chip's reliable output rate, eliminating fill samples.

PPS timepulse 25->1 Hz (period 1_000_000 us): the timepulse is only
used for RTC clock sync via GPIO interrupt, so 1 Hz is sufficient
and reduces unnecessary ISR load.

commit.message:
refactor: replace prediction engine with calibrated GBM model

BREAKING: Prediction engine completely overhauled to fix absurd scenarios
(e.g. NVDA predicted at $1000/share for bull scenario).

Root causes fixed:
- HMM with only 252 days of data produced unreliable regime estimates
- No bounds on p5/p95 percentiles → extreme values passed to UI
- √T extrapolation (scalePercentiles) amplified errors exponentially
- Raw 1-year historical mean used as future expected return

New architecture:
- Primary model: Calibrated GBM with Student-t quantiles (ν=5)
  - Closed-form percentiles, no Monte Carlo needed
  - Drift shrinkage: blend historical μ with 8% equity prior (w=dataYears/10)
  - Damped volatility for horizons >2yr (mean-reversion à la Fama & French)
  - Hard sanity bounds: [-80%,+100%]/year, [-95%,+1000%] total
- Secondary: Block bootstrap for short horizons (≤6 months)
- HMM demoted to informational regime display only
- GARCH retired from ensemble (no value beyond ~20 trading days)

Expected results (NVDA ~$140, σ≈50%, 12mo):
- Before: Bear -70%, Bull +150% (or worse)
- After: Bear ~-38%, Bull ~+58%

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(layers): ensure NEFLayer init tensors are on CPU

When centers or encoder_kwargs["train_data"] arrive on a GPU device
(e.g. from ConvNEFPipeline.fit on CUDA), make_encoders and the bias
computation would mix GPU and CPU tensors, causing a RuntimeError.

Move all intermediate tensors (encoders, encoder_centers,
selected_centers, all_centers) to CPU during __init__. The registered
buffers are relocated to the target device later via .to(device).

commit.message:
fix: clock-set spin loop — store now not egps.time in state

set_from_egps stored egps.time (GPS PVT timestamp) in LocationState
after a successful RTC set. If EGPS_TIME was stale (GPS time >10s in
the past), (now - egps.time) > LOCATION_DIFF was true again on the
very next call, causing an infinite re-trigger loop.

Fix: store 'now' (wall-clock at time of set) so the 10s cooldown is
measured from when the set actually happened. Also rate-limit the
NaiveDateTime::from_timestamp_millis failure path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Build improvements (#142)

Addresses two recent feedback items:
- zig pollutes the user's .cache/zig directory, which may grow large
- Selene manifests take too long to write

The first item is managed by providing zig with a cache dir, which
subsequently sets `ZIG_LOCAL_CACHE_DIR` in the subprocess environment.

The second is managed by providing a `complete_manifest` bool flag to
`build()`. When True, the manifest aims to be a complete picture of the
build stages, for use with sharing and diagnosis. When False (default),
less information is written to the manifest. At current this reduced
information is in the form of stripping input artifact or intermediate
artifacts in the manifest, keeping only the final binary.
